top_categories.workout_context: Eroica California's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 110 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Eroica California's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
68ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Eroica California, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
At mile 60 of 110, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Eroica California. Train it until it feels boring.
7,500ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
7,500ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Eroica California, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 110 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 7,500ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Eroica California’s 110 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Eroica California. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at Eroica California demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride mixed terrain at race-day cadence weekly. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 110 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 110 miles of mixed terrain in Cambria, Central Coast, California, USA.
